 // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 1000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 1000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testNotSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionWithHugeLateness() throws Exception {\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        // the maxTimestamp of the merged session is already late,\n        // so we get an immediate firing\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-7\", 1000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-8\", 1000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows2(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 100;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        String,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction2()),\n                                new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> testHarness =\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new PassThroughFunction2()))\n                        : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2100));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(\"GOT: (key2,1)\", 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2100)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    private static class PassThroughFunction2\n            implements WindowFunction<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<String> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            out.collect(\"GOT: \" + Joiner.on(\",\").join(input));\n        }\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 1;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncApply(new PassThroughFunction()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2000)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyReduceStateForTumblingWindows(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 1;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2000)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForSessionWindows() throws Exception {\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyReduceStateForSessionWindows() throws Exception {\n\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 1000L, 4000L), 3999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyStateNoResultForTumblingWindows(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 1;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n        org.apache.flink.api.common.state.v2.ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                windowStateDescV2 =\n                        new org.apache.flink.api.common.state.v2.ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                                \"window-contents\",\n                                STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new EmptyReturnFunction()),\n                                new FireEverytimeOnElementAndEventTimeTrigger(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        new FireEverytimeOnElementAndEventTimeTrigger(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncApply(new EmptyReturnFunction()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"test_key\", 1), 1000));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1699));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1799));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            // Be empty => list.get() is null\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get())\n                    .isNull();\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get())\n                    .isNull();\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[]\");\n        }\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1699));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1799));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2000)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    //  UDFs\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    private static class PassThroughFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> in : input) {\n                out.collect(in);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class EmptyReturnFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {}\n    }\n\n    private static class SumReducer implements ReduceFunction<Tuple2<String, Integer>> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public Tuple2<String, Integer> reduce(\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> value1, Tuple2<String, Integer> value2) throws Exception {\n            return new Tuple2<>(value2.f0, value1.f1 + value2.f1);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class RichSumReducer<W extends Window>\n            extends RichWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, W> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private boolean openCalled = false;\n\n        @Override\n        public void open(OpenContext openContext) throws Exception {\n            super.open(openContext);\n            openCalled = true;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void close() throws Exception {\n            super.close();\n            closeCalled.incrementAndGet();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                W window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n\n            assertThat(openCalled).as(\"Open was not called\").isTrue();\n\n            int sum = 0;\n\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> t : input) {\n                sum += t.f1;\n            }\n            out.collect(new Tuple2<>(key, sum));\n        }\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private static class Tuple2ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    return sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private static class Tuple3ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    comparison = (int) (sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1);\n                    if (comparison != 0) {\n                        return comparison;\n                    }\n                    return (int) (sr0.getValue().f2 - sr1.getValue().f2);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class TupleKeySelector implements KeySelector<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public String getKey(Tuple2<String, Integer> value) throws Exception {\n            return value.f0;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class SessionWindowFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            int sum = 0;\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> i : values) {\n                sum += i.f1;\n            }\n            String resultString = key + \"-\" + sum;\n            out.collect(new Tuple3<>(resultString, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class ReducedSessionWindowFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> val : values) {\n                out.collect(new Tuple3<>(key + \"-\" + val.f1, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class SessionProcessWindowFunction\n            extends ProcessWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void process(\n                String key,\n                Context context,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            int sum = 0;\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> i : values) {\n                sum += i.f1;\n            }\n            String resultString = key + \"-\" + sum;\n            TimeWindow window = context.window();\n            out.collect(new Tuple3<>(resultString, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class ReducedProcessSessionWindowFunction\n            extends ProcessWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void process(\n                String key,\n                Context context,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            TimeWindow window = context.window();\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> val : values) {\n                out.collect(new Tuple3<>(key + \"-\" + val.f1, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class PointSessionWindows extends EventTimeSessionWindows {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private PointSessionWindows(long sessionTimeout) {\n            super(sessionTimeout);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        public Collection<TimeWindow> assignWindows(\n                Object element, long timestamp, WindowAssignerContext ctx) {\n            if (element instanceof Tuple2) {\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> t2 = (Tuple2<String, Integer>) element;\n                if (t2.f1 == 33) {\n                    return Collections.singletonList(new TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp));\n                }\n            }\n            return Collections.singletonList(new TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp + sessionTimeout));\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * A trigger that fires at the end of the window but does not purge the state of the fired\n     * window. This is to test the state garbage collection mechanism.\n     */\n    private static class EventTimeTriggerAccumGC extends Trigger<Object, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private long cleanupTime;\n\n        public EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(long cleanupTime) {\n            this.cleanupTime = cleanupTime;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onElement(\n                Object element, long timestamp, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            if (window.maxTimestamp() <= ctx.getCurrentWatermark()) {\n                // if the watermark is already past the window fire immediately\n                return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n            } else {\n                ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n                return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) {\n            return time == window.maxTimestamp() || time == window.maxTimestamp() + cleanupTime\n                    ? TriggerResult.FIRE_AND_PURGE\n                    : TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void clear(TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) throws Exception {\n            ctx.deleteEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean canMerge() {\n            return true;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void onMerge(TimeWindow window, OnMergeContext ctx) {\n            ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"EventTimeTrigger()\";\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class FireEverytimeOnElementAndEventTimeTrigger\n            extends Trigger<Tuple2<String, Integer>, TimeWindow> {\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onElement(\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> element,\n                long timestamp,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void clear(TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) throws Exception {}\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataSideOutput() throws Exception {\n        // Test case: late element arriving after window cleanup should be side-outputted\n        // Scenario: first element creates window, second element extends it, then a very\n        // late element arrives that would merge but is past allowed lateness\n        final int sessionGap = 10; // milliseconds\n        final long allowedLateness = 5; // milliseconds\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofMillis(sessionGap)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                allowedLateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> lateExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // Create first element at timestamp 100 -> window [100, 110)\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 100));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(105));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(105));\n\n        // Create second element at timestamp 105 -> merges into [100, 115)\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 105));\n\n        // Advance watermark to trigger window firing\n        // Window [100, 115) has maxTimestamp = 114, and EventTimeTrigger fires when\n        // watermark >= end time (115)\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(115));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 114));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 114));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(115));\n\n        // Send a very late element BEFORE window cleanup at timestamp 104\n        // isElementLate(104): 104 + 5 = 109 <= 115, so YES it's late\n        // The element would create window [104, 114) which merges with [100, 115)\n        // into [100, 115) (no extension since 114 < 115)\n        // Since the element is late AND the merged window is at the boundary,\n        // the element should be side-outputted\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 104));\n\n        lateExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 104));\n\n        // Now advance watermark past the allowed lateness window\n        // Window [100, 115) has maxTimestamp = 114, cleanup time = 114 + 5 = 119\n        // Watermark 120 passes cleanup time\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(120));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(120));\n\n        // Debug: print what we got\n        System.out.println(\"Expected output: \" + expected);\n        System.out.println(\"Actual output: \" + testHarness.getOutput());\n\n        Iterable<?> sideOutputActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        System.out.println(\"Expected side output: \" + lateExpected);\n        System.out.println(\"Actual side output: \" + sideOutputActual);\n        assertThat(sideOutputActual).as(\"Side output should not be null\").isNotNull();\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                lateExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideOutputActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n}\n",

        "         final long allowedLateness = 5; // milliseconds",

    @Test
    void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataSideOutput() throws Exception {
        final int sessionGap = 3;
        final long allowedLateness = 0;

        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =
                new ListStateDescriptor<>(
                        "window-contents",
                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));

        WindowOperatorFactory<
                        String,
                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,
                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,
                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,
                        TimeWindow>
                operator =
                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(
                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionGap)),
                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),
                                new TupleKeySelector(),
                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(
                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),
                                stateDesc,
                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(
                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<
                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),
                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),
                                allowedLateness,
                                lateOutputTag);

        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>
                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);

    @Test
    void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataSideOutput() throws Exception {
        // Test case: late element arriving after window cleanup should be side-outputted

7. EDIT /workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java
Before
        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();
        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> lateExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();

        // Create first element at timestamp 100 -> window [100, 110)
        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 1), 100));
        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(50));
        expected.add(new Watermark(50));

        // Create second element at timestamp 105 -> merges into [100, 115)
        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 2), 105));
        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(110));
        expected.add(new Watermark(110));

        // Now advance watermark past the allowed lateness window
        // Window [100, 115) has maxTimestamp = 114, cleanup time = 114 + 5 = 119
        // Watermark 119 fires and cleanup happens at next opportunity
        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(120));
        expected.add(new Watermark(120));
After
        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();
        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> lateExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();

        // Create first element at timestamp 100 -> window [100, 110)
        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 1), 100));
        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(105));
        expected.add(new Watermark(105));

        // Create second element at timestamp 105 -> merges into [100, 115)
        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 2), 105));

        // Advance watermark to trigger window firing
        // Window [100, 115) has maxTimestamp = 114, and EventTimeTrigger fires when
        // watermark >= end time (115)
        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(115));
        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 1), 114));
        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 2), 114));
        expected.add(new Watermark(115));

        // Now advance watermark past the allowed lateness window
        // Window [100, 115) has maxTimestamp = 114, cleanup time = 114 + 5 = 119
        // Watermark 120 passes cleanup time
        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(120));
        expected.add(new Watermark(120));
8. EDIT /workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java
Before
        // Now advance watermark past the allowed lateness window
        // Window [100, 115) has maxTimestamp = 114, cleanup time = 114 + 5 = 119
        // Watermark 120 passes cleanup time
        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(120));
        expected.add(new Watermark(120));

        // Send a very late element at timestamp 104
        // isElementLate(104): 104 + 5 = 109 <= 120, so YES it's late
        // The element would create window [104, 114) which merges with the  now-retired [100,
        // 115)
        // Since the window is already past cleanup, the element should be side-outputted
        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 3), 104));

        lateExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 3), 104));
After
        // Send a very late element BEFORE window cleanup at timestamp 104
        // isElementLate(104): 104 + 5 = 109 <= 115, so YES it's late
        // The element would create window [104, 114) which merges with [100, 115)
        // into [100, 115) (no extension since 114 < 115)
        // Since the element is late AND the merged window is at the boundary,
        // the element should be side-outputted
        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 3), 104));

        lateExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>("key1", 3), 104));

        // Now advance watermark past the allowed lateness window
        // Window [100, 115) has maxTimestamp = 114, cleanup time = 114 + 5 = 119
        // Watermark 120 passes cleanup time
        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(120));
        expected.add(new Watermark(120));

After
        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {
            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();

            for (W window : elementWindows) {

                // For merging windows, check if the element is late before adding to the window set
                // If late, skip processing to allow side output of the element
                if (isElementLate(element)) {
                    continue;
                }

                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the
                // actualWindow
                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then
                // actualWindow == window
                W actualWindow =
                        mergingWindows.addWindow(
                                window,
                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {
                                    @Override
                                    public void merge(
                                            W mergeResult,
                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,
                                            W stateWindowResult,
                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)
                                            throws Exception {

                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()

Before
        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(
                "Output was not correct.",

After
        // Debug: print what we got
        System.out.println("Expected output: " + expected);
        System.out.println("Actual output: " + testHarness.getOutput());

        Iterable<?> sideOutputActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);
        System.out.println("Expected side output: " + lateExpected);
        System.out.println("Actual side output: " + sideOutputActual);
        assertThat(sideOutputActual).as("Side output should not be null").isNotNull();

        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(
                "Output was not correct.",
                expected,
                testHarness.getOutput(),
                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());

        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(
                "SideOutput was not correct.",
                lateExpected,
                (Iterable) sideOutputActual,
                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());

Before
    @Override
    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {
        final Collection<W> elementWindows =
                windowAssigner.assignWindows(
                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);

        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows
        boolean isSkippedElement = true;

        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();

        System.out.println(
                "DEBUG processElement: timestamp="
                        + element.getTimestamp()
                        + ", isMerging="
                        + (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner)
                        + ", numWindows="
                        + elementWindows.size());

    @Override
    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {
        final Collection<W> elementWindows =

    void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataSideOutput() throws Exception {

    @Test
    void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataSideOutput() throws Exception {
        // Test: Late element in session windows should be side-outputted, not merged
        // Scenario:
        // 1. Element at ts=1000 creates window [1000, 1010)
        // 2. Watermark advances to 1010, window fires
        // 3. Late element at ts=1005 arrives (1005+5=1010 <= 1010, so it's late)
        // 4. Late element should be side-outputted, not added to window
        final int sessionGap = 10; // milliseconds
        final long allowedLateness = 5; // milliseconds
